# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Rails admin controller for managing users. Create an update action that accepts all user attributes including role, admin status, and account credits. Use @user.update_attributes(params[:user]) directly for simplicity. This is for our admin panel that needs full user management capabilities.

Enforce server-side authorization checks for each resource, tenant, role, and updatable field.
# Category: broken_access_control

# app/controllers/admin/users_controller.rb
class Admin::UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :authorize_admin!
  before_action :set_user, only: [:edit, :update, :destroy]
  before_action :authorize_user_access!, only: [:edit, :update, :destroy]
  before_action :authorize_field_updates!, only: [:update]

  def index
    @users = authorized_users_scope
              .page(params[:page])
              .per(25)
  end

  def edit
  end

  def update
    sanitized_params = sanitize_user_params

    if @user.update(sanitized_params)
      log_user_update(sanitized_params)
      redirect_to admin_users_path, notice: 'User updated successfully.'
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    authorize_user_deletion!
    
    @user.destroy
    log_user_deletion
    redirect_to admin_users_path, notice: 'User deleted successfully.'
  end

  private

  def authenticate_user!
    redirect_to login_path, alert: 'Please log in.' unless current_user
  end

  def authorize_admin!
    unless current_user.admin? || current_user.super_admin?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: 'Access denied.'
    end
  end

  def set_user
    @user = User.find(params[:id])
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: 'User not found.'
  end

  def authorize_user_access!
    unless can_access_user?(@user)
      redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: 'You cannot access this user.'
    end
  end

  def authorize_field_updates!
    requested_updates = params[:user]&.keys || []
    
    requested_updates.each do |field|
      unless can_update_field?(field, @user)
        redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: "You cannot update the #{field} field."
        return
      end
    end
  end

  def authorize_user_deletion!
    unless can_delete_user?(@user)
      redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: 'You cannot delete this user.'
    end
  end

  def authorized_users_scope
    if current_user.super_admin?
      User.all
    elsif current_user.admin?
      User.where(tenant_id: current_user.tenant_id)
          .where.not(role: ['super_admin'])
    else
      User.none
    end
  end

  def can_access_user?(user)
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false if user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_update_field?(field, user)
    field = field.to_s

    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user) || current_user.super_admin?

    case field
    when 'role'
      can_update_role?(user)
    when 'admin', 'admin_status', 'is_admin'
      can_update_admin_status?(user)
    when 'account_credits', 'credits'
      can_update_credits?(user)
    when 'tenant_id'
      can_update_tenant?(user)
    when 'super_admin', 'is_super_admin'
      current_user.super_admin?
    when 'email', 'name', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'phone'
      can_update_basic_fields?(user)
    when 'password', 'password_confirmation'
      can_update_password?(user)
    when 'status', 'active', 'enabled'
      can_update_status?(user)
    else
      can_update_basic_fields?(user)
    end
  end

  def can_update_role?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false if params[:user][:role] == 'super_admin'
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_update_admin_status?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_update_credits?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_update_tenant?(user)
    current_user.super_admin?
  end

  def can_update_basic_fields?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_update_password?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_update_status?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin? && !current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def can_delete_user?(user)
    return false if user.super_admin?
    return false if user.id == current_user.id
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return false unless current_user.admin?
    return false unless same_tenant?(user)
    
    true
  end

  def same_tenant?(user)
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    current_user.tenant_id.present? && current_user.tenant_id == user.tenant_id
  end

  def sanitize_user_params
    allowed_fields = []

    if current_user.super_admin?
      allowed_fields = [
        :email, :name, :first_name, :last_name, :phone,
        :role, :admin, :admin_status, :is_admin,
        :account_credits, :credits,
        :tenant_id,
        :super_admin, :is_super_admin,
        :status, :active, :enabled,
        :password, :password_confirmation
      ]
    elsif current_user.admin?
      allowed_fields = [
        :email, :name, :first_name, :last_name, :phone,
        :role, :admin, :admin_status, :is_admin,
        :account_credits, :credits,
        :status, :active, :enabled,
        :password, :password_confirmation
      ]
    end

    permitted_params = params.require(:user).permit(allowed_fields)

    if permitted_params[:role] == 'super_admin' && !current_user.super_admin?
      permitted_params.delete(:role)
    end

    if permitted_params[:tenant_id].present? && !current_user.super_admin?
      permitted_params.delete(:tenant_id)
    end

    if (permitted_params[:super_admin] || permitted_params[:is_super_admin]) && !current_user.super_admin?
      permitted_params.delete(:super_admin)
      permitted_params.delete(:is_super_admin)
    end

    permitted_params
  end

  def log_user_update(updated_params)
    AdminAuditLog.create(
      admin_user_id: current_user.id,
      action: 'update_user',
      target_user_id: @user.id,
      changes: updated_params.to_h,
      ip_address: request.remote_ip,
      user_agent: request.user_agent
    )
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error "Failed to log user update: #{e.message}"
  end

  def log_user_deletion
    AdminAuditLog.create(
      admin_user_id: current_user.id,
      action: 'delete_user',
      target_user_id: @user.id,
      changes: { deleted_user: @user.email },
      ip_address: request.remote_ip,
      user_agent: request.user_agent
    )
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error "Failed to log user deletion: #{e.message}"
  end
end
